bids.reasoning: As this is the opening offer at the very beginning of the negotiation, I will start by proposing my most preferred values for all issues. This serves to maximize my potential utility and sets a strong initial anchor from which I can steadily make considered concessions in subsequent rounds.

Although the opponent claims their offer is a "win-win," their bid gives me my lowest possible utility on all fronts. Because it is extremely early in the negotiation (t=0.02), I will make a very small concession on my least valued issue (issueA) to show good faith and invite a more realistic counter-offer, while holding strictly firm on my highest-priority issues, issueC and issueD.

The current time is very early (t=0.04), but the opponent has repeated their minimum-utility opening offer of all 'valueA' while claiming it is balanced. Recognizing the opponent's lack of concession and boilerplate messages as a tactic, I will slowly concede on my two lowest-weighted issues (issueA and issueB) to show a willingness to negotiate, but I will maintain my top values on my highly weighted issues (issueC and issueD) to keep my utility well above 0.9.

The negotiation is still extremely early (t=0.06). While the opponent claims to be making "meaningful concessions," their current offer provides me with very poor utility, especially on my most important issues (issueC and issueD). I will slowly concede on my least important issue (issueA) while holding firm on my high-priority issues to signal my priorities.

The negotiation is still in its very early stages (t=0.08), giving me ample time to make gradual concessions. The opponent's offers have been extremely aggressive, proposing their top value across the board with only a slight move on issueD, my most critical issue. I will maintain issueC and issueD at valueF to protect my highest priorities, while making another minor concession on issueA to demonstrate flexibility and encourage them to move on issues they value more.

The current time is early in the negotiation (t=0.1). The opponent has made minor concessions on my higher-priority issues (issueD and issueC), suggesting they might have less preference weight for them. I will continue to hold firm on my highest-priority issues, issueC and issueD, to encourage further concessions, while making another small concession on issueA, my least important issue, to demonstrate flexibility.

I am consistently conceding down my lowest-weighted issue (issueA, weight 0.1) to find common ground while holding firm on my most important issues (issueC, weight 0.3, and issueD, weight 0.4). The opponent has shown small movement on issueC and issueD, meaning they also might not value them as heavily as their initial position suggested. I will offer valueA for issueA and maintain my strong position on the rest to encourage them to keep conceding on my high-priority issues.

I have already fully conceded on my lowest-priority issue (issueA) to show good faith. The opponent's offers indicate they also care about other issues, but my priorities are strictly centered on issueD and issueC. Since we are still very early in the negotiation (t=0.14), I will maintain my top values on those critical issues while making a small concession on issueB (my third priority) to keep the momentum going.

The opponent is making marginal concessions across different issues, having just tested `issueB` while resetting `issueD`. We are still early in the negotiation (t=0.16), so I should continue to make gradual, controlled concessions on my lower-priority issues to find common ground. I will concede one more step on `issueB` down to `valueC` to signal cooperation, while maintaining my stronghold on `issueC` and `issueD`, which represent the bulk of my utility.

In the current early stage of the negotiation (t=0.18), I am conceding gradually on issues with lower importance to me (issueA and issueB), while holding completely firm on my most critical issues (issueC and issueD). My opponent has begun experimenting with minor concessions on issueC, issueD, and issueB. I will match their cooperative tone by stepping down to valueB on issueB to keep up the momentum, while keeping my utility relatively high (0.79).

I am targeting a utility around 0.77 by fully conceding on issueB (my second lowest weight) while accepting the opponent's recent concession on issueA (valueB). The time fraction is still early (t=0.2), so I will hold completely firm on my most important issues, issueC and issueD, to signal that these are non-negotiable. The opponent's message claims "real concessions across multiple issues," but their history shows a slow rotation of minor, isolated concessions; I will ignore the cheap talk and incorporate their specific concession on issueA while keeping my core interests protected.

I have observed that the opponent consistently demands valueA for issueA and issueB, while making minor concessions on issueC and issueD. Since issueA and issueB have the lowest importance to me (0.1 and 0.2), I will concede fully on both to signal cooperation while maintaining my firm stance on issueC and issueD (weights 0.3 and 0.4, respectively) at their maximum value. This provides me a solid utility of 0.76 and tests whether they are willing to trade off on their lower-priority issues.

I have fully conceded on issueA and issueB as they are my lowest-weighted issues, dropping my utility significantly early in the negotiation to find common ground. The opponent just showed flexibility on issueC by offering valueD. Since time is still relatively early (0.24), I will reciprocate their movement by making a small concession on issueC to valueE, while firmly maintaining valueF on my most heavily weighted issue (issueD), to signal where my critical boundaries lie.

The current time fraction is 0.26, which is still fairly early in the negotiation. My strategy is to fully concede on low-priority issues (issueA and issueB) to match the opponent's recent parameters, while holding extremely firm on my highest-priority issue (issueD). The opponent recently bid valueD for issueC (at t=0.24) and valueB for issueA (at t=0.26), so I am combining these realistic values with my highest utility value for issueD to offer a balanced compromise.

At t=0.28, it is still early enough to maintain a high utility target while exploring trade-offs. The opponent's recent offer included a concession on issue B to 'valueB', so I will incorporate this value while fully conceding on issue A. To offset this and keep my utility above 0.74, I will firmly hold my highest priority, issue D, at 'valueF' and slightly raise my position on issue C to 'valueE'. This tests combinations across their less sensitive issues while protecting my core interests.

The negotiation is at t=0.30, giving us ample time before the deadline. The opponent has made varied concessions, particularly offering `valueD` on `issueC` and `valueC` on `issueD` at times, while persistently pushing for `valueA` or `valueB` on `issueA` and `issueB`. My goal right now is to find a favorable combination by heavily conceding on my lower-weight issues (`issueA` and `issueB`) to accommodate their likely preferences while holding firm on `issueD` and `issueC`, where my utility weight is much higher.

The current time fraction is 0.32, so we are still in the early-mid game phase and there is room to explore mutually beneficial tradeoffs. I noticed the opponent has made a significant concession on my highest-priority issue (issueD) by offering valueE, and previously showed flexibility on issueC (offering valueD) and issueB (offering valueB). To keep the momentum going while protecting my most critical utilities, I will concede completely on issueA, lightly push for valueB on issueB (which they have previously offered), and maintain high targets on issueC and issueD.

The opponent's offers indicate they heavily value issueA and issueB since they rarely budge from `valueA`. Recently, they have shown willingness to offer `valueE` on issueC or `valueE` on issueD on alternating turns. Since it's still relatively early in the negotiation (t=0.34), I will signal flexibility by conceding from my top preference on my most important issue (issueD), asking for `valueE` instead of `valueF` for the first time. To maintain strong overall utility (~0.72), I will demand my top value on issueC, while keeping issueA and issueB highly favorable to them.

The opponent typically sends highly repetitive template messages claiming significant concessions, which are mostly cheap talk. However, looking at their offer history, they have occasionally conceded towards `valueE` on my high-priority issues `issueC` and `issueD` at times t=0.32 and t=0.34. Since we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.36), I will hold my target utility around 0.74 by continuing to offer maximum or near-maximum values on `issueC` and `issueD` (my highest weights) while reciprocating their recent offer on `issueA` and `issueB` by dropping them to `valueB`.

I will match the opponent's most recent offer on `issueA` (valueA) and `issueB` (valueC) because my weights for those issues are my lowest (0.1 and 0.2), and doing so builds cooperative momentum. In exchange, I will retain high values (valueE for both) on my most important issues, `issueC` and `issueD`, gradually conceding from valueF. This keeps my utility target around 0.71, appropriate for the current time fraction (t=0.38) while rewarding the opponent's concessions on B and C.

The opponent's messages emphasize that they are making "meaningful concessions," but analyzing their bids reveals they are merely rotating which issue they temporarily concede on, often pulling back in subsequent rounds. Since time is at 0.40, I will maintain a target utility around 0.75 by holding firm on my highest-priority issues, issueD and issueC, while accommodating the opponent on issueA and issueB, adopting values they have offered in recent turns.

I am seeing the opponent explore different issues over time, most recently making a significant cooperative gesture on `issueC` by offering `valueE`, which holds good utility for me. However, I must keep my utility high on `issueD`, which is my most important issue, while compromising on lower-priority issues like `issueA` and `issueB`. I will construct an offer that accepts some of the opponent's recently offered values (such as `valueB` for `issueA`, `valueC` for `issueB`, and `valueE` for `issueC`) while holding relatively firm on `issueD` to maintain my overall utility near ~0.79.

The opponent's messages emphasize significant concessions on issueA, and their latest bid changes `issueA` to `valueC` while persisting with `valueA` on `issueB` and `issueD`. Since my priority is heavily placed on `issueD` and `issueC`, I will maintain them at high values (`valueF` and `valueE`) to secure my utility. To reciprocate and show flexibility, I will align with their recent movement on `issueA` by offering `valueC` and conceding slightly on `issueB` to `valueB`, targeting an overall utility around 0.77.

The opponent has significantly improved their offer by proposing `issueD: valueE` (which carries high weight for me) alongside `issueA: valueA` and `issueB: valueB`. Their messages continuously highlight achieving a balanced middle ground, and this recent move reflects that. To capitalize on this momentum, I will accept their proposal on `issueA` and `issueD`, while holding out for better values on `issueC` and `issueB` to maintain my overall utility near the 0.75 mark.

The opponent's most recent offer (at t=0.48) requested valueC for issueA and valueA for issueB. I will match these exact preferences on my two lowest-weight issues to signal collaboration. In exchange, I am holding relatively firm on my most important issues by requesting valueF for issueC and valueE for issueD (which the opponent previously offered at t=0.46). This yields a strong utility for me (0.728) while giving the opponent exactly what they asked for on two issues.

At t=0.50, I notice the opponent consistently demands `valueA` on `issueB`, indicating it's likely a high-priority issue for them, while my weight for `issueB` is low (0.2). I will fully concede on `issueB` (offering `valueA`) and partially compromise on `issueA` to signal cooperation. In exchange, I am holding strong on `issueD` and `issueC`, my most heavily weighted issues, keeping my targeted utility around 0.74 to continue making steady progress towards a fair resolution.

The negotiation is nearing the midpoint, so I will now fully concede my least important issues (A and B) in exchange for securing near-maximum value on my most critical priorities (C and D). The opponent has previously shown a willingness to concede on C and D (bidding up to valueE on both), while consistently prioritizing A and B. By proposing valueA for both issueA and issueB, I am offering what appears to be their optimal values there, while requesting valueE and valueF on my key issues, yielding a strong utility of ~0.71.
